page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CL processor based on the x86_64 architecture. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each vCPU maps directly to a dedicated physical core, eliminating resource contention from hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

The high memory density relative to the compute allocation makes this instance class suitable for memory-intensive workloads. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and a moderate 10 Gbps network baseline are tradeoffs to consider for I/O-heavy applications. This profile is designed for large in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CL processor. Utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture, it provides a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration to ensure dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading overhead. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. It does not contain local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, such as large-scale in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications requiring significant memory capacity relative to compute resources.
